comment_text.ud3.3:1.4: The narrative employs a unique scheme in introducing the repetitions: tedha, tete, teme.
comment_text.ud3.3:8.3: Here the Buddha invents the concept of “faint praise”.
comment_text.ud3.3:15.2: Ānandajoti has “about them” for te here, but in parallel phrases we find no, so this must be the second person singular pronoun.

root_text.ud3.3:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud3.3:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena yasojappamukhāni pañcamattāni bhikkhusatāni sāvatthiṁ anuppattāni honti bhagavantaṁ dassanāya.
root_text.ud3.3:1.4: Tedha kho āgantukā bhikkhū nevāsikehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ paṭisammodamānā senāsanāni paññāpayamānā pattacīvarāni paṭisāmayamānā uccāsaddā mahāsaddā ahesuṁ.
root_text.ud3.3:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.ud3.3:2.2: “ke panete, ānanda, uccāsaddā mahāsaddā kevaṭṭā maññe macchavilope”ti?
root_text.ud3.3:2.3: “Etāni, bhante, yasojappamukhāni pañcamattāni bhikkhusatāni sāvatthiṁ anuppattāni bhagavantaṁ dassanāya.
root_text.ud3.3:2.4: Tete āgantukā bhikkhū nevāsikehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ paṭisammodamānā senāsanāni paññāpayamānā pattacīvarāni paṭisāmayamānā uccāsaddā mahāsaddā”ti.
root_text.ud3.3:3.1: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paṭissutvā yena te bhikkhū tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā te bhikkhū etadavoca:
root_text.ud3.3:3.3: “Evamāvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato ānandassa paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho te bhikkhū bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.ud3.3:4.1: “Kiṁ nu tumhe, bhikkhave, uccāsaddā mahāsaddā, kevaṭṭā maññe macchavilope”ti?
root_text.ud3.3:4.2: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā yasojo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud3.3:4.3: “imāni, bhante, pañcamattāni bhikkhusatāni sāvatthiṁ anuppattāni bhagavantaṁ dassanāya.
root_text.ud3.3:4.4: Teme āgantukā bhikkhū nevāsikehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ paṭisammodamānā senāsanāni paññāpayamānā pattacīvarāni paṭisāmayamānā uccāsaddā mahāsaddā”ti.
root_text.ud3.3:4.5: “Gacchatha, bhikkhave, paṇāmemi vo; na vo mama santike vatthabban”ti.
root_text.ud3.3:5.1: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paṭissutvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā senāsanaṁ saṁsāmetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena vajjī tena cārikaṁ pakkamiṁsu.
root_text.ud3.3:5.2: Vajjīsu anupubbena cārikaṁ caramānā yena vaggumudā nadī tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā vaggumudāya nadiyā tīre paṇṇakuṭiyo karitvā vassaṁ upagacchiṁsu.
root_text.ud3.3:6.1: Atha kho āyasmā yasojo vassūpagato bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.ud3.3:6.2: “bhagavatā mayaṁ, āvuso, paṇāmitā atthakāmena hitesinā, anukampakena anukampaṁ upādāya.
root_text.ud3.3:6.3: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, tathā vihāraṁ kappema yathā no viharataṁ bhagavā attamano assā”ti.
root_text.ud3.3:6.4: “Evamāvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato yasojassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.ud3.3:6.5: Atha kho te bhikkhū vūpakaṭṭhā appamattā ātāpino pahitattā viharantā tenevantaravassena sabbeva tisso vijjā sacchākaṁsu.
root_text.ud3.3:7.1: Atha kho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ yathābhirantaṁ viharitvā yena vesālī tena cārikaṁ pakkāmi.
root_text.ud3.3:7.2: Anupubbena cārikaṁ caramāno yena vesālī tadavasari.
root_text.ud3.3:7.3: Tatra sudaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.ud3.3:8.1: Atha kho bhagavā vaggumudātīriyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ cetasā ceto paricca manasi karitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.ud3.3:8.2: “ālokajātā viya me, ānanda, esā disā, obhāsajātā viya me, ānanda, esā disā;
root_text.ud3.3:8.3: yassaṁ disāyaṁ vaggumudātīriyā bhikkhū viharanti gantuṁ appaṭikūlāsi me manasi kātuṁ.
root_text.ud3.3:8.4: Pahiṇeyyāsi tvaṁ, ānanda, vaggumudātīriyānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ santike dūtaṁ:
root_text.ud3.3:9.1: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paṭissutvā yena aññataro bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud3.3:9.2: “ehi tvaṁ, āvuso, yena vaggumudātīriyā bhikkhū tenupasaṅkama; upasaṅkamitvā vaggumudātīriye bhikkhū evaṁ vadehi:
root_text.ud3.3:10.1: “Evamāvuso”ti kho so bhikkhu āyasmato ānandassa paṭissutvā—
root_text.ud3.3:10.2: seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evamevaṁ—mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ antarahito vaggumudāya nadiyā tīre tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ purato pāturahosi.
root_text.ud3.3:10.3: Atha kho so bhikkhu vaggumudātīriye bhikkhū etadavoca:
root_text.ud3.3:11.1: “Evamāvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū tassa bhikkhuno paṭissutvā senāsanaṁ saṁsāmetvā pattacīvaramādāya—
root_text.ud3.3:11.2: seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evamevaṁ—vaggumudāya nadiyā tīre antarahitā mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ bhagavato sammukhe pāturahesuṁ.
root_text.ud3.3:11.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā āneñjena samādhinā nisinno hoti.
root_text.ud3.3:11.5: “katamena nu kho bhagavā vihārena etarahi viharatī”ti?
root_text.ud3.3:12.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando abhikkantāya rattiyā, nikkhante paṭhame yāme, uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud3.3:12.2: “abhikkantā, bhante, ratti; nikkhanto paṭhamo yāmo; ciranisinnā āgantukā bhikkhū;
root_text.ud3.3:13.1: Dutiyampi kho āyasmā ānando abhikkantāya rattiyā, nikkhante majjhime yāme, uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud3.3:14.1: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā ānando abhikkantāya rattiyā, nikkhante pacchime yāme, uddhaste aruṇe, nandimukhiyā rattiyā uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud3.3:15.1: Atha kho bhagavā tamhā samādhimhā vuṭṭhahitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.ud3.3:15.2: “sace kho tvaṁ, ānanda, jāneyyāsi ettakampi te nappaṭibhāseyya.
root_text.ud3.3:15.3: Ahañca, ānanda, imāni ca pañca bhikkhusatāni sabbeva āneñjasamādhinā nisīdimhā”ti.
root_text.ud3.3:16.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud3.3:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud3.3:1.3: Now at that time five hundred mendicants headed by Yasoja arrived at Sāvatthī to see the Buddha.
translation_text.ud3.3:1.4: At that, those visiting mendicants, while exchanging pleasantries with the resident mendicants, preparing their lodgings, and putting away their bowls and robes, made a colossal racket.
translation_text.ud3.3:2.1: Then the Buddha said to Venerable Ānanda,
translation_text.ud3.3:2.2: “Ānanda, who’s making that colossal racket? You’d think it was fishermen hauling in a catch!”
translation_text.ud3.3:2.3: “Sir, those five hundred mendicants headed by Yasoja have arrived at Sāvatthī to see the Buddha.
translation_text.ud3.3:2.4: It’s those visiting mendicants who, while exchanging pleasantries with the resident mendicants, preparing their lodgings, and putting away their bowls and robes, made a colossal racket.”
translation_text.ud3.3:2.5: “Well then, Ānanda, in my name tell those mendicants that
translation_text.ud3.3:3.1: “Yes, sir,” Ānanda replied. He went to those mendicants and said,
translation_text.ud3.3:3.3: “Yes, reverend,” replied those mendicants. Then they rose from their seats and went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to them,
translation_text.ud3.3:4.1: “Mendicants, what’s with that colossal racket? You’d think it was fishermen hauling in a catch!”
translation_text.ud3.3:4.2: When he said this, Venerable Yasoja said to the Buddha,
translation_text.ud3.3:4.3: “Sir, these five hundred mendicants have arrived at Sāvatthī to see the Buddha.
translation_text.ud3.3:4.4: It’s these visiting mendicants who, while exchanging pleasantries with the resident mendicants, preparing their lodgings, and putting away their bowls and robes, made a colossal racket.”
translation_text.ud3.3:4.5: “Go away, mendicants, I dismiss you. You are not to stay in my presence.”
translation_text.ud3.3:5.1: “Yes, sir,” replied those mendicants. They got up from their seats, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on their right. They set their lodgings in order and left, taking their bowls and robes.
translation_text.ud3.3:5.2: Traveling stage by stage in the land of the Vajjis, they arrived at the Vaggumudā River. They built leaf huts near the riverbank and there they entered the rainy season.
translation_text.ud3.3:6.1: Then Venerable Yasoja, having entered the rainy season, addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.ud3.3:6.2: “Out of sympathy, reverends, the Buddha dismissed us, wanting what’s best for us.
translation_text.ud3.3:6.3: Come, let us live in such a way that the Buddha would be pleased with us.”
translation_text.ud3.3:6.5: Then those mendicants, living alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute, all realized the three knowledges in that same rainy season.
translation_text.ud3.3:7.1: When the Buddha had stayed in Sāvatthī as long as he pleased, he set out for Vesālī.
translation_text.ud3.3:7.2: Traveling stage by stage, he arrived at Vesālī,
translation_text.ud3.3:7.3: where he stayed in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.ud3.3:8.1: Then, having applied his mind to encompassing the minds of the mendicants staying on the bank of the river Vaggumudā, the Buddha said to Venerable Ānanda,
translation_text.ud3.3:8.2: “A light, it appears to me, has arisen in this quarter, Ānanda; a brightness has arisen.
translation_text.ud3.3:8.3: I’m not put off at the thought of going to where the mendicants are staying on the bank of the river Vaggumudā.
translation_text.ud3.3:8.4: Send a message to those mendicants:
translation_text.ud3.3:8.5: ‘Venerables, the teacher summons you. He wants to see you.’”
translation_text.ud3.3:9.1: “Yes, sir,” Ānanda replied. He went to one of the mendicants and said,
translation_text.ud3.3:9.2: “Please, Reverend, go to the mendicants staying on the bank of the river Vaggumudā and say to them,
translation_text.ud3.3:9.3: ‘Venerables, the teacher summons you. He wants to see you.’”
translation_text.ud3.3:10.2: Then, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, he vanished from the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof, and reappeared in front of those mendicants on the bank of the river Vaggumudā.
translation_text.ud3.3:10.3: Then he said to those mendicants,
translation_text.ud3.3:10.4: “Venerables, the teacher summons you. He wants to see you.”
translation_text.ud3.3:11.1: “Yes, reverend,” replied those mendicants. They set their lodgings in order and took their bowls and robes.
translation_text.ud3.3:11.2: Then, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, they vanished from the bank of the river Vaggumudā, and reappeared in the presence of the Buddha in the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.ud3.3:11.3: But at that time the Buddha was sitting immersed in imperturbable meditation.
translation_text.ud3.3:11.5: “What kind of meditation is the Buddha practicing right now?”
translation_text.ud3.3:11.8: They all sat in imperturbable meditation.
translation_text.ud3.3:12.1: And then, as the night was getting late, in the first watch of the night, Venerable Ānanda got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha and said,
translation_text.ud3.3:12.2: “Sir, the night is getting late. It is the first watch of the night, and the visiting mendicants have been sitting long.
translation_text.ud3.3:12.3: Sir, please greet the visiting mendicants.”
translation_text.ud3.3:13.1: For a second time, as the night was getting late, in the middle watch of the night, Ānanda got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha and said,
translation_text.ud3.3:13.2: “Sir, the night is getting late. It is the second watch of the night, and the visiting mendicants have been sitting long.
translation_text.ud3.3:13.5: Sir, please greet the visiting mendicants.”
translation_text.ud3.3:13.6: But for a second time the Buddha kept silent.
translation_text.ud3.3:14.1: For a third time, as the night was getting late, in the last watch of the night, as dawn stirred, bringing joy to the night, Ānanda got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha and said,
translation_text.ud3.3:14.2: “Sir, the night is getting late. It is the last watch of the night; dawn stirs, bringing joy to the night, and the visiting mendicants have been sitting long.
translation_text.ud3.3:14.7: Sir, please greet the visiting mendicants.”
translation_text.ud3.3:15.1: Then the Buddha emerged from that immersion and addressed Ānanda,
translation_text.ud3.3:15.2: “If you’d known, Ānanda, you wouldn’t have said so much.
translation_text.ud3.3:15.3: Both I and these five hundred mendicants have been sitting in imperturbable meditation.”
translation_text.ud3.3:16.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud3.3:17.1: “A mendicant who has beaten <j>the thorn of sensuality—
translation_text.ud3.3:17.4: trembles not at pleasure and pain.”

variant_text.ud3.3:4.5: paṇāmemi vo → vo panāmemi (bj); vo paṇāmemi (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)
variant_text.ud3.3:15.2: nappaṭibhāseyya → na paṭibhāseyya (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1); nappaṭibhāyeyya (an10.96:1 [96. Kokanudasutta]); nappaṭibheyya (?)
